# Known Go-fallback ↔ Rust mister-core divergences (shrink-only quarantine).
#
# Each line: <verb>/<session>/<labelset> <rust_verdict> <go_verdict>
# meaning the Go fallback (local_fallback*.go) returns go_verdict where the Rust
# oracle returns the STRICTER rust_verdict — i.e. the fallback is more permissive
# than the oracle for this input.
#
# This file is consumed by TestDifferentialFallbackNeverMorePermissive. The test
# FAILS if:
#   (a) a divergence appears that is NOT listed here          (new drift), or
#   (b) a listed divergence changes verdicts                  (drift moved), or
#   (c) a listed case no longer diverges                      (stale entry —
#       delete the line; the quarantine must only ever shrink).
#
# CURRENT STATE: EMPTY. With a valid corpus, the Go fallback is never more
# permissive than the Rust oracle across all 588 (verb × session × label) cases —
# i.e. exact parity in the safe direction. Any future drift fails the test as
# NEW DRIFT and must be fixed in local_fallback*.go, not quarantined here.
#
# HISTORY (why this was once large): the first revision of the corpus fed
# mister-core invalid IFC provenance values ("local"/"external") that its parser
# rejects, AND sent no lease. Both made Rust return artificial denies (a parse
# error and a built-in default-deny, respectively) that core.Evaluate surfaces as
# fail-closed denies — so the test was comparing the Go fallback against Rust
# *errors*, not Rust *policy*, fabricating ~250 phantom divergences. The corpus
# now uses valid provenance (user / external_package) and supplies the production
# default lease (lease.DefaultLease(), empty forbidden_verbs). With realistic
# input the divergence set is empty. A reviewer (Codex) caught the provenance bug.
